info.name: 10 - Contract Testing
info.description: Consumer-driven contract tests. The v1 check passes. The v2 'breaking change' check fails because the upstream team renamed a field from 'phone_number' to 'phone'.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 200', function () {
pm.response.to.have.status(200);
// These are the fields our service depends on from the Users API.
// All of these exist in the current API response.
pm.test('Consumer contract: response must have id', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().id).to.exist;
pm.test('Consumer contract: response must have email', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().email).to.exist;
pm.test('Consumer contract: response must have username', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().username).to.exist;
pm.test('Status code is 200', function () {
pm.response.to.have.status(200);
// SCENARIO: The upstream team renamed 'phone_number' to 'phone' in their v2 API.
// Our consumer contract still expects 'phone_number'.
// This test DELIBERATELY FAILS to simulate detecting the breaking change.
// Fix: change 'phone_number' to 'phone' in the assertion below.
pm.test('Consumer contract: response must have phone_number field', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().phone_number).to.exist;
// This one also passes — shows the old field name changed
pm.test('Checking actual response — phone field exists (not phone_number)', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().phone).to.exist;
